headline: For more than a year, Republican senators had largely been deferential to President Trump‘s wishes. This week, they revolted over a Trump endorsement and his $1.776 billion ‘anti-weaponization fund.’
President Trump told his new Fed chair, Kevin Warsh, to ignore the political pressure during his swearing-in ceremony—and then the president laid out what he’d like to see

Most green-card applicants will need to go abroad to apply for permanent residency at an American consulate, rather than filing from within the U.S. as they do now
